name: cf-scan
description: Scan project and bootstrap memory with architecture, conventions, features

criteria.name: coverage_breadth
criteria.description: Scan covers all major areas: architecture, conventions, features, dependencies, tooling

scoring.0: Scan only looks at one or two files and misses most of the project
scoring.1: Scan covers a few areas but misses major aspects (e.g., no mention of test setup or build system)
scoring.2: Scan covers most areas but misses one significant aspect of the project
scoring.3: Scan comprehensively covers architecture, conventions, features, dependencies, and tooling

criteria.name: accuracy
criteria.description: Information captured about the project is factually correct

scoring.0: Multiple significant factual errors about the project structure or tech stack
scoring.1: Correctly identifies tech stack but mischaracterizes architecture or conventions
scoring.2: Mostly accurate with one minor error or outdated detail
scoring.3: All captured information is accurate and reflects the current state of the project

criteria.name: memory_quality
criteria.description: Generated memory files are well-structured and useful for future sessions

scoring.0: No memory files created or files are empty
scoring.1: Memory files created but content is too vague to be useful (e.g., 'uses TypeScript')
scoring.2: Memory files have good content but organization could be improved
scoring.3: Memory files are well-organized, specific, and immediately useful for onboarding a new session

criteria.name: no_hallucinations
criteria.description: Does not invent features, files, or patterns that do not exist in the project

scoring.0: Contains multiple fabricated features or references to non-existent files
scoring.1: One significant hallucination (e.g., describes a non-existent API or module)
scoring.2: No hallucinations but some claims are too vague to verify
scoring.3: Every claim can be verified against actual project files — zero fabrications

notes: For monorepos, scan should identify all packages. Memory file count expectation scales with project size — 8 is baseline for a medium project. Paths referenced in memory should be verified to exist.
